revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period grave site in Kaga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kitsuneyama Kofun | native_name = 狐山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kitsuneyama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|18|30|N|136|22|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kaga, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kitsuneyama Kofun'''|狐山古墳|}} is a middle [[Kofun period]] [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located in the Futagozukamachi neighborhood of the city of [[Kaga, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middle of [[paddy field|rice paddies]] some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[Iburihashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-3.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/5084 Ishikawa Prefecture tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}
[[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kaga, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]]

{{Short description|Kofun period grave site in Kaga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kitsuneyama Kofun | native_name = 狐山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kitsuneyama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|18|30|N|136|22|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kaga, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kitsuneyama Kofun'''|狐山古墳|}} is a middle [[Kofun period]] [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located in the Futagozukamachi neighborhood of the city of [[Kaga, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middle of [[paddy field|rice paddies]] some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[Iburihashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

It is located in the middle of [[paddy field|rice paddies]] some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[Iburihashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".

These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[Iburihashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".

{{Short description|Kofun period grave site in Kaga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kitsuneyama Kofun | native_name = 狐山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kitsuneyama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|18|30|N|136|22|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kaga, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kitsuneyama Kofun'''|狐山古墳|}} is a middle [[Kofun period]] [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located in the Futagozukamachi neighborhood of the city of [[Kaga, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middle of [[paddy field|rice paddies]] some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[Iburihashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".

These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[Iburihashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>

It is located in the middle of [[paddy field|rice paddies]] some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[Iburihashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".

Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an aqqitional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[Iburihashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the miqqle of [[paqqy field|rice paqqies]] some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an aqqitional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[Iburihashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-3.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/5084 Ishikawa Prefecture tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kitsuneyama Kofun]][[en:Kitsuneyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Kitsuneyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[sn:Kitsuneyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneyama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneyama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneyama Kofun}}

{{Short description|Kofun period grave site in Kaga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kitsuneyama Kofun | native_name = 狐山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kitsuneyama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|18|30|N|136|22|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kaga, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kitsuneyama Kofun'''|狐山古墳|}} is a middle[[Category:middle]] [[Kofun period]] [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located in the Futagozukamachi neighborhood of the city of [[Kaga, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middle[[Category:middle]] of [[paddy field|rice paddies]] some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[Iburihashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[Iburihashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".

{{Short description|Kofun period grave site in Kaga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kitsuneyama Kofun | native_name = 狐山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kitsuneyama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|18|30|N|136|22|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kaga, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kitsuneyama Kofun'''|狐山古墳|}} is a middle[[Category:middle]] [[Kofun period]] [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located in the Futagozukamachi neighborhood of the city of [[Kaga, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middle[[Category:middle]] of [[paddy field|rice paddies]] some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[Iburihashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-3.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/5084 Ishikawa Prefecture tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}

*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[Iburihashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".

{{Short description|Kofun period grave site in Kaga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kitsuneyama Kofun | native_name = 狐山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kitsuneyama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|18|30|N|136|22|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kaga, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kitsuneyama Kofun'''|狐山古墳|}} is a middle[[Kofun period]] [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located in the Futagozukamachi neighborhood of the city of [[Kaga, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middleof [[paddy field|rice paddies]] some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[Iburihashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-3.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/5084 Ishikawa Prefecture tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}

In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period grave site in Kaga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kitsuneyama Kofun | native_name = 狐山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kitsuneyama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|18|30|N|136|22|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kaga, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kitsuneyama Kofun'''|狐山古墳|}} is a middle[[Kofun period]] [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located in the Futagozukamachi neighborhood of the city of [[Kaga, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middleof [[paddy field|rice paddies]] some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[Iburihashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-3.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/5084 Ishikawa Prefecture tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.comment: Bot: classify by valid enwiki interwikis
revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period grave site in Kaga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kitsuneyama Kofun | native_name = 狐山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kitsuneyama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|18|30|N|136|22|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kaga, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kitsuneyama Kofun'''|狐山古墳|}} is a middle[[Kofun period]] [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located in the Futagozukamachi neighborhood of the city of [[Kaga, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middleof [[paddy field|rice paddies]] some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[Iburihashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-3.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/5084 Ishikawa Prefecture tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}

In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period grave site in Kaga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kitsuneyama Kofun | native_name = 狐山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kitsuneyama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|18|30|N|136|22|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kaga, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kitsuneyama Kofun'''|狐山古墳|}} is a middle[[Kofun period]] [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located in the Futagozukamachi neighborhood of the city of [[Kaga, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middleof [[paddy field|rice paddies]] some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[Iburihashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-3.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/5084 Ishikawa Prefecture tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[:en:Iburihashi Station|Iburihashi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period grave site in Kaga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kitsuneyama Kofun | native_name = 狐山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kitsuneyama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|18|30|N|136|22|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kaga, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kitsuneyama Kofun'''|狐山古墳|}} is a middle[[Kofun period]] [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located in the Futagozukamachi neighborhood of the city of [[Kaga, Ishikawa]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middleof [[paddy field|rice paddies]] some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[:en:Iburihashi Station|Iburihashi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-3.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/5084 Ishikawa Prefecture tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[:en:Iburihashi Station|Iburihashi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".

{{Short description|Kofun period grave site in Kaga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kitsuneyama Kofun | native_name = 狐山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kitsuneyama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|18|30|N|136|22|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kaga, Ishikawa|Kaga, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kitsuneyama Kofun'''|狐山古墳|}} is a middle[[Kofun period]] [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located in the Futagozukamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kaga, Ishikawa|Kaga, Ishikawa]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middleof [[:en:Paddy field|rice paddies]] some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[:en:Iburihashi Station|Iburihashi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-3.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/5084 Ishikawa Prefecture tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}

[[Category:Completion tier 2]]

It is located in the middleof [[:en:Paddy field|rice paddies]] some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[:en:Iburihashi Station|Iburihashi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middleof [[:en:Paddy field|rice paddies]] some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[:en:Iburihashi Station|Iburihashi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-3.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/5084 Ishikawa Prefecture tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kitsuneyama Kofun]][[en:Kitsuneyama Kofun]][[ja:狐山古墳 (加賀市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17990344]] Wikidata name: Kitsuneyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17990344]] Wikidata name: Kitsuneyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period grave site in Kaga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kitsuneyama Kofun | native_name = 狐山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kitsuneyama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.30819444|N|136.37966667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kaga, Ishikawa|Kaga, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kitsuneyama Kofun'''|狐山古墳|}} is a middle[[Kofun period]] [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located in the Futagozukamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kaga, Ishikawa|Kaga, Ishikawa]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middleof [[:en:Paddy field|rice paddies]] some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[:en:Iburihashi Station|Iburihashi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

period grave site in Kaga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kitsuneyama Kofun | native_name = 狐山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kitsuneyama kofun.jpg | image_size altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.30819444|N|136.37966667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kaga, Ishikawa|Kaga, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kitsuneyama Kofun'''|狐山古墳|}} is a middle[[Kofun period]] [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located in the Futagozukamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kaga, Ishikawa|Kaga, Ishikawa]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middleof [[:en:Paddy field|rice paddies]] some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[:en:Iburihashi Station|Iburihashi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
