From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kitsuneyama Kofun]][[en:Kitsuneyama Kofun]][[ja:狐山古墳 (加賀市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kitsuneyama Kofun]][[en:Kitsuneyama name: Kitsuneyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17990344]] Wikidata name: Kitsuneyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period grave site in Kaga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kitsuneyama Kofun | native_name = 狐山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kitsuneyama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.30819444|N|136.37966667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kaga, Ishikawa|Kaga, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kitsuneyama Kofun'''|狐山古墳|}} is a middle[[Kofun period]] [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located in the Futagozukamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kaga, Ishikawa|Kaga, Ishikawa]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middleof [[:en:Paddy field|rice paddies]] some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[:en:Iburihashi Station|Iburihashi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from [[:en:Iburihashi Station|Iburihashi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".

In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. ==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middleof {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=rice paddies}} some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}, including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from {{ill|Iburihashi Station|en|Iburihashi Station|WD=Q3611075|lt=Iburihashi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|WD=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".

From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".

name: Kitsuneyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period grave site in Kaga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kitsuneyama Kofun | native_name = 狐山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kitsuneyama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.30819444|N|136.37966667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|Kaga, Ishikawa|WD=Q120103|lt=Kaga, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kitsuneyama Kofun'''|狐山古墳|}} is a middle[[Kofun period]] [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located in the Futagozukamachi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|Kaga, Ishikawa|WD=Q120103|lt=Kaga, Ishikawa}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middleof {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=rice paddies}} some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}, including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from {{ill|Iburihashi Station|en|Iburihashi Station|WD=Q3611075|lt=Iburihashi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|WD=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|WD=Q6573264|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)}}

==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middleof {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=rice paddies}} some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}, including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from {{ill|Iburihashi Station|en|Iburihashi Station|WD=Q3611075|lt=Iburihashi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|WD=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|WD=Q6573264|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-3.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/5084 Ishikawa Prefecture tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from {{ill|Iburihashi Station|en|Iburihashi Station|WD=Q3611075|lt=Iburihashi Station|1=Iburihashi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|WD=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line|1=Hokuriku Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".

{{Short description|Kofun period grave site in Kaga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kitsuneyama Kofun | native_name = 狐山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kitsuneyama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.30819444|N|136.37966667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|Kaga, Ishikawa|WD=Q120103|lt=Kaga, Ishikawa|1=Kaga, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kitsuneyama Kofun'''|狐山古墳|}} is a middle[[Kofun period]] [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located in the Futagozukamachi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|Kaga, Ishikawa|WD=Q120103|lt=Kaga, Ishikawa|1=Kaga, Ishikawa}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middleof {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=rice paddies|1=Paddy field}} some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from {{ill|Iburihashi Station|en|Iburihashi Station|WD=Q3611075|lt=Iburihashi Station|1=Iburihashi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|WD=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line|1=Hokuriku Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".

{{Short description|Kofun period grave site in Kaga, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kitsuneyama Kofun | native_name = 狐山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kitsuneyama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitsuneyama Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.30819444|N|136.37966667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|Kaga, Ishikawa|qid=Q120103|lt=Kaga, Ishikawa|1=Kaga, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|qid=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kitsuneyama Kofun'''|狐山古墳|}} is a middle[[Kofun period]] [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located in the Futagozukamachi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|Kaga, Ishikawa|qid=Q120103|lt=Kaga, Ishikawa|1=Kaga, Ishikawa}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|qid=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middleof {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|qid=Q842623|lt=rice paddies|1=Paddy field}} some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from {{ill|Iburihashi Station|en|Iburihashi Station|qid=Q3611075|lt=Iburihashi Station|1=Iburihashi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|qid=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line|1=Hokuriku Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|qid=Q6573264|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-3.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/5084 Ishikawa Prefecture tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201536 |title= 狐山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kitsuneyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the middleof {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|qid=Q842623|lt=rice paddies|1=Paddy field}} some 200 meters north of the city center of Kaga, and is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Kaga Plains. The tumulus is orientated east-west, and has a total length of 54 meters. The posterior circular portion is 29 meters in diameter and the anterior rectangular portion is 25 meters in width. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat with an average width of 10 meters. During construction work in 1952, a 2.7 meter long {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} was discovered during construction work. Inside were the bone of a youth, along with an extraordinary amount of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, including iron weapons, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments armor, jewelry (silver bands, gold and copper balls, round and tubular beads). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During [[archaeological excavation]] of the moat from 1973 to 1974, ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' fragments and many stones thought to be ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered. These artifacts date the tumulus to the later half of the fifth century, and from the quality and quantity of items, this must have been the tomb of a local king with a close relationship to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
*Total length 55.8 meters *Anterior rectangular portion; 26.5meter width, 5.1 meter height *Posterior circular portion: 26.5 meter diameter, 6.5 meter height
In a subsequent survey, an additional 36 ''kofun'' (one keyhole-shaped, and 35 dome-shaped) have been discovered in the surrounding area. Currently, some of the excavated artifacts from the site are stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}. A shelter has been built over the sarcophagus so that it can be viewed through a window. The tumulus is located about seven minutes by car from {{ill|Iburihashi Station|en|Iburihashi Station|qid=Q3611075|lt=Iburihashi Station|1=Iburihashi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|qid=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line|1=Hokuriku Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1960, the site has also been registered as an "Ishikawa Prefecture Cultural Property".
